title: [lora_l2.c:134] hwid_len check could allow negative values as size_t
description: In generate_eui64() at line 134-144, hwid_len is ssize_t. The check 'hwid_len <= 0' handles negative returns correctly, but on line 141 the cast '(size_t)hwid_len > sizeof(hwid)' is redundant since we already validated hwid_len <= 0. If hwinfo_get_device_id() returns 0 (empty hwid), the code proceeds to hash an empty buffer. While SHA-256 of empty input is well-defined, an empty hardware ID may indicate a hardware issue that should be flagged more explicitly. The LOG_ERR on line 138 ('No hardware ID available') won't fire since 0 > 0 is false - it should check hwid_len < 1.
close_reason: Fixed: Split hwid_len check into separate < 0 and == 0 conditions for clarity. Now explicitly returns the error code on failure, handles zero-length hwid as -ENODEV. The lora_l2.c.obj compiled successfully; overall build fails due to unrelated toolchain issue with arm-zephyr-eabi-ar/ranlib.
